title: alnus viridis var. repens
intro: Alnus viridis var. repens, also known as green alder or creeping alder, is a variety of the green alder plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa. The leaves and bark of the Alnus viridis var. repens plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which is thought to provide pain relief and reduce fever. Alnus viridis var. repens can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
